question: Choose the correct word: 'The old tree trunk was completely ________, which is why birds nested inside it.' (empty inside)
choices: flat
solid
spherical
hollow

question: Choose the correct word: 'The sculpture was so ________ that they couldn't get it through the door of the exhibition centre.' (very large in size)
choices: enormous
curved
spherical
pointed

question: Choose the correct word: 'They found several ________ coins with their new metal detector.' (made of brown metal)
choices: bone
silk
bronze
ivory

question: Choose the correct word: 'This intriguing artefact is ________ in shape, but no one knows what its purpose was.' (round like a circle)
choices: solid
spherical
hollow
circular

question: Choose the correct word: 'The music was so ________ that everyone laughed when they played it.' (sentimental and lacking quality)
choices: golden
geometric
tasteful
cheesy

question: The expression 'Would it be OK if we...?' is used in informal negotiation to ________.
choices: make a final decision after all options have been discussed
ask for clarification about something that was said earlier
reject a suggestion politely without offering an alternative
make a suggestion about an alternative course of action

question: The expression 'I thought perhaps we could...' is used to ________.
choices: reject a suggestion and propose something else instead
make a gentle suggestion without being too direct
demand that everyone accepts a particular plan immediately
ask for more time before making a final decision

question: The expression 'I can see what you're saying, but...' is used to ________.
choices: accept a suggestion and express complete satisfaction
make a suggestion using a conditional structure
politely disagree with or not fully accept a suggestion
make a final decision and bring the negotiation to a close

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'The circles in the field are so perfect that they _____ by a farmer — farmers don't have the necessary equipment.'
choices: must have been made
might have been made
would have been made
can't have been made

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'A wealthy person _____ to use the artefact — it was found in a royal chamber.'
choices: can't have been planning
might not have been planning
must have been planning
wouldn't have been planning

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'No one really knows what they _____ for.' (passive, uncertain past use)
choices: must have been used
can't have been used
might have been used
would have been used

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'He _____ carrying out the robbery — I was with him all evening.' (negative certainty)
choices: might have been
must have been
would have been
can't have been

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'I knew the background to the situation, so I understood how she felt.'
choices: Having known the background to the situation, I understood how she felt
Knowing the background to the situation, I understood how she felt
Not knowing the background to the situation, I understood how she felt
To know the background to the situation, I understood how she felt

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'She looked at the diamond carefully and said, I think it is a fake.'
choices: She was looking at the diamond carefully, saying it was a fake
Having looked carefully at the diamond, she said it was a fake
Looking at the diamond carefully, she said, I think it is a fake
Not looking carefully, she said the diamond was a fake

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'She didn't know the city well, so she got lost.'
choices: Not knowing the city well, she got lost
Having not known the city well, she got lost
Knowing not the city well, she got lost
She didn't know the city well, getting lost

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'He had watched the film several times, so he remembered every scene.'
choices: Watching the film several times, he remembered every scene
Not having watched the film, he remembered every scene
He watched the film, having remembered every scene
Having watched the film several times, he remembered every scene

question: Choose the correct word to complete: 'The ________ refers to the actors taking part in a performance.'
choices: cast
dialogue
scene
set

question: Choose the correct phrase: 'When he ________, nobody laughed — it completely ruined the joke.'
choices: played the lead
delivered the punchline
did a stand-up routine
cracked a few jokes

question: Choose the correct word to complete: 'The ________ is the storyline of a play, film, or book.'
choices: episode
cast
set
plot

question: Choose the correct word to complete: 'I would never have the confidence to go up on a stage and tell jokes alone — to do a ________.'
choices: sitcom
feature-length film
stand-up routine
scene

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'The audience loved the performance — the costumes and setting were very ________.' (decorated or designed well)
choices: tasty
insipid
tasteful
comical

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'All's Well That Ends Well is a ________ comedy by Shakespeare.' (timeless/iconic)
choices: historical
classical
classic
historic

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'The final week of rehearsals seemed ________ — the first night of the play was awful.' (no value)
choices: invaluable
childlike
comical
worthless

question: Read the following excerpt: 'familiarity with a work of art enables you to appreciate it more.' According to this statement, familiarity with a work of art ________.
choices: always reduces your enjoyment of something you have seen before
makes you want to share your opinions with others online
causes you to focus only on the ending rather than the whole story
helps you to appreciate the work of art more fully

question: What was the key finding of the follow-up study regarding spoilers?
choices: Spoilers only improved enjoyment at the very end of the story
Participants who had read spoilers found the stories more engaging even halfway through
The second group of readers preferred shorter stories without spoilers
Spoilers had absolutely no effect on how much readers enjoyed the stories

question: What does the phrase 'gone are the days' suggest in the context of the Internet and viewing habits?
choices: The Internet has made it easier to watch content at a set time
People no longer discuss what they watch with anyone at all
Those past habits of watching together no longer exist today
The days when television was popular have completely ended

question: According to information about Jordan's constitutional system, a 'constitutional monarchy' means ________.
choices: the monarch rules alone without any involvement from the government
the prime minister holds more power than the king in all matters
the people vote directly for all government decisions
the monarch follows the constitution and shares power with the government

question: In Jordan, the prime minister 'chooses the cabinet, subject to parliamentary approval.' This means ________.
choices: the prime minister selects the cabinet without needing any approval
the cabinet is chosen by the senate without involving the prime minister
parliament must agree to the prime minister's choice of cabinet
the King chooses the cabinet on the recommendation of parliament

question: According to the world parliament facts, the world's oldest parliament that still exists is the Althing in Iceland. It was founded in ________.
choices: the year 1066 CE
the year 500 BCE
the year 1215 CE
the year 930 CE

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', the woods are described as 'lovely, dark and deep'. This suggests that the speaker finds the woods ________.
choices: frightening and impossible to enter safely
ugly and not worth stopping to look at
inviting and beautiful, yet also mysterious
familiar and comforting because he owns them

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', what does the repetition of the final two lines suggest?
choices: The speaker is too tired to continue and decides to rest in the woods
The speaker is looking for a specific location in the forest
The speaker has a long journey ahead and many responsibilities to fulfil
The speaker is afraid that night will fall before he reaches the village

question: The INTRODUCTION of an article should ________.
choices: give a detailed description of every character in the story
provide a full summary of the plot from beginning to end
grab the reader's attention and outline what the article is about
list all the themes and discuss them in alphabetical order

question: In a review article, the phrase 'I was blown away by the performance of...' is used to ________.
choices: summarise the main points of the article
express strong personal admiration for an actor's performance
criticise the quality of the acting in the production
describe the setting and costumes used in the performance

question: The phrase 'alliteration' in poetry means ________.
choices: the repetition of the same vowel sound in different words in a line
the use of the same word at the beginning and end of a poem
the repetition of the same ending sound in two or more words
the use of the same letter or sound at the beginning of nearby words

question: The phrase 'the cabinet' in the context of government refers to ________.
choices: the building where parliament meets to debate legislation
the important ministers who control government policy
the committee responsible for writing new laws
the group of advisors who assist the prime minister privately
